title: Matched Compute
objective: Evaluate specialists vs monolith under explicit training/inference compute parity constraints with guarded interpretation.

logic: quality_delta_rule = null when Acc@1 and MRR are tied with monolith within 1e-12.
compute_parity_rule = positive when training parity ratio is within tolerance.
end_to_end_efficiency_rule = regression when routed specialist inference FLOPs exceed 2.0x monolith.
overall = inconclusive when fairness parity passes but quality is null and inference-cost rule regresses.

evidence: Training compute parity achieved (ratio=1.0000 within tolerance=0.0100).
Acc@1 and MRR are tied at 1.0000 for monolith, semantic routing, and Kalman fusion in this run.
null.notes: No quality advantage is demonstrated under this benchmark slice.

evidence: Training parity is satisfied, but the run cannot support an overall superiority claim due to inference asymmetry.
inconclusive.notes: Quality parity plus materially higher inference FLOPs prevents claim closure.

evidence: Semantic routing and Kalman routing require 2.20x and 2.30x inference FLOPs proxy versus monolith.
regression.notes: Inference-cost regression relative to monolith remains unresolved.

notes: Outcome labels are rule-based and computed conservatively.
Null outcomes are preserved as first-class evidence.
This track is completed as an evidence artifact but does not close the specialists-vs-monolith superiority hypothesis.
